name: role
summary: Add a WordPress user role on Ready (idempotent).
description: Scaffolds a Ready listener that calls add_role() if the role does not already exist. Roles persist in wp_options once added.
problem: You need a custom user role with specific capabilities — and you don't want to hand-edit add_role() calls scattered across activation hooks.
appliesWhen: You need a single named role with a known set of capabilities
Idempotent runtime registration is acceptable (vs activation-only)
avoidWhen: You only need to add capabilities to an existing role (modify $role->add_cap() instead)
You need a per-environment role set with versioning (build a small migration system)
The role membership is dynamic per-user (look at WordPress capabilities/filters instead)
examples: Add an acme_collaborator role for the integration
Register a billing_admin role with billing-specific capabilities
tradeoffs: One class plus a listener registration. Runs every Ready dispatch but is idempotent (skipped when the role exists). Removing capabilities later requires a separate migration class.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that adds the role on Ready
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Edit capabilities() to grant the actual capabilities the role needs

name.aiHint: PascalCase. Convention: name after the role concept (Collaborator, BillingAdmin).

roleLabel.description: Display name shown in admin UI

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register the listener in
